comment.body:
if i take the casino metaphore, it says that the House is buying the downside bets, and selling the upside bets.
if the house prices the downside bets above its fair value, it will give a positive EV, so house wins on the long run, assuming downside bets print.

and if we assume that upside bets also print, the House should price the upside bets lower than its fair value... but its not what happens!
it prices above its fair value
comment.raw_body:
if i take the casino metaphore, it says that the House is buying the downside bets, and selling the upside bets.
if the house prices the downside bets above its fair value, it will give a positive EV, so house wins on the long run, assuming downside bets print.

and if we assume that upside bets also print, the House should price the upside bets lower than its fair value... but its not what happens!
it prices above its fair value

comment.body:
Thanks Alma for clarifying. It also makes sense from a political read of the fed structure. 

I fully expect 4 dissent votes in this FOMC. Waller, Miran, Bowman are Trump lapdogs and political appointees, they will surely put their vote on record demanding a 50bps cut. Lisa Cook is untouchable right now lol and she can even put her vote for a hike and there is nothing anyone can do anything about it. 

Would not make for a smooth consensus vote and Powell himself is on his way out and he will not fully bend his knee to the emperor. 25bps is at best what might happen. I doubt dovish commentary comes out given the 3 dissenting votes who might argue economy has deteriorated. Lisa cook might argue inflation will roar back. Either way market will not hear the lullaby it needs to hear :) 

25bps with hawkish tone- market will not like and might pin in place at centroid or fall to primary downside at 6504
50bps with hawkish tone on jobs- market should fall on worry and get to downside target of 6415 ( downside max)
25bps with dovish tone- market can get to the upside max Alma pointed at 6700

I found it in the monthly post

comment.body:
Alma, I'm grateful for your modeling and read on the market and "house". I'm newish to options. When you moved beyond BSM to add skewness and kurtosis, which model did you use? 
I think you mentioned it in a chat some time back, and you posted a paper. Is it something like:
"a non-rough path-dependent model and a two-factor Markovian model that outperform their rough counterparts in capturing SPX smiles between one week and three years, with only 3 to 4 parameters"
From: https://arxiv.org/html/2401.03345v2
Thank you.
